content_blocks.text:
this is a new chapter
353
12 getting gui
Face it, you need to make GUIs. If you’re building applications that other people 
are going to use, you need a graphical interface. If you’re building programs for yourself, you 
want a graphical interface. Even if you believe that the rest of your natural life will be spent 
writing server-side code, where the client user interface is a web page, sooner or later you’11 
need to write tools, and you’11 want a graphical interface. Sure, command-line apps are retro, 
but not in a good way. They’re weak, inflexible, and unfriendly. We’11 spend two chapters 
working on GUIs, and learn key Java language features along the way including Event 
Handling and Inner Classes. In this chapter, we’11 put a button on the screen, and make it 
do something when you click it. We’11 paint on the screen, we’11 display a jpeg image, and we’11 
even do some animation. 
A Very Graphic 
Story
1 heard your 
ex-wife could only cook 
command-line meals.
Wow! This looks great. 
1 guess presentation 
really is everything.
